summary: Binary toggle family with a button-backed switch atom, a canonical SwitchField path, and thin purpose-switch wrappers.
philosophy: Teach SwitchField as the canonical labeled toggle surface and keep raw Switch as the low-level escape hatch.
Keep checked-state semantics in core and field wiring in shared helpers so React and Vue stay aligned.
Treat the button-backed role=switch model as an explicit family choice rather than an incidental implementation detail.
Keep purpose-switch wrappers thin and semantic so they add meaning without becoming a second switch implementation.

useWhen: The UI needs a binary on/off control with visible labeling and optional description or error support.
The product flow maps clearly to FeatureToggle, PreferenceSwitch, or PermissionSwitch and benefits from self-documenting purpose metadata.
The team wants React and Vue switch behavior to stay aligned through shared core helpers and one contract file.
avoidWhen: A different control such as checkbox, radio, or tabs is the more honest interaction model.
The implementation only needs visual styling and cannot own naming or described-by wiring for a raw custom layout.
The team expects switch error handling to behave like text-input invalid semantics rather than described-by support.

automationCovers: Shared switch contract coverage across React and Vue for checked state, disabled behavior, visible-label wiring, description wiring, and error wiring.
Local atom tests for direct raw-switch naming and size-class behavior in both adapters.
Storybook introduction and taxonomy tests in both apps.
manualReviewRequired: Real browser and assistive-technology validation for checked-state announcements, focus visibility, and toggle feel.
Future decisions about which switch stories should join stricter automated accessibility gates.
Direct raw aria-labelledby usage if the family wants dedicated local proof beyond the current shared-contract surface.
notes: Switch completed a first-pass audit and now has an explicit spec-backed family contract.
The family still sits outside the wave-1 central semantic registry even though purpose wrappers already emit useful metadata.

openQuestions: Which Switch-family stories should later join automated accessibility gates?
Should direct raw aria-labelledby usage be added to package-level tests, or remain documented without dedicated local assertions?
